title: Motherboards, CPUs, Firmware, TPM, Expansion, and Cooling
description: A small studio needs a board and CPU repair decision after failed boot, thermal throttling, firmware changes, and an expansion-card upgrade with uncertain lane, power, and security requirements. Learners cumulatively produce board-level compatibility and boot evidence packet through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Relate motherboard form factor, socket, chipset, buses, slots, headers, storage interfaces, ports, and replaceable components to requirements.
Compare architecture, cores, threads, clock behavior, cache, integrated features, virtualization support, power, thermals, and workload evidence.
Trace power-on, firmware settings, POST evidence, boot selection, UEFI, Secure Boot, update risk, reset, and recovery boundaries.
